kp7:2.1: But when a meal with plentiful food & drink is served,
kp7:2.4: Such is the kamma of living beings.
kp7:3.1: So those who feel sympathy for their dead relatives give timely donations of proper food & drink
kp7:4.3: And those who have gathered there,
kp7:4.4: the assembled shades of the relatives,
kp7:5.1: with appreciation, give their blessing for the plentiful food & drink:
kp7:5.4: because of whom we have gained [this gift].
kp7:6.2: and the donors are not without reward!”
kp7:6.3: For there [in their realm] there’s no farming,
kp7:7.3: They live on what is given here,
kp7:7.4: hungry ghosts whose time here is done.
kp7:8.1: As water raining on a hill
kp7:8.3: even so does what is given here
kp7:9.3: even so does what is given here
kp7:10.1: “He gave to me, she acted on my behalf,
kp7:10.2: they were my relatives, companions, friends”:
kp7:10.3: Offerings should be given for the dead
kp7:10.4: when one reflects thus on things done in the past.
kp7:11.4: whose relatives persist in that way.
kp7:12.1: But when this offering is given,
kp7:12.3: it works for their long-term benefit
kp7:13.1: In this way the proper duty to relatives has been shown,
kp7:13.2: great honor has been done to the dead,
kp7:13.3: and monks have been given strength:
kp7:13.4: The merit you’ve acquired isn’t small.
